channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Joan Laporta é reeleito presidente do Barcelona até 2031
item.description: Joan Laporta was re-elected president of Barcelona with 68,18% of the votes, defeating Victor Font and securing his mandate until 2031. The election saw participation from 42% of the club’s members (48,480 votes), and Laporta emphasized that the focus will be completing the partial reconstruction of Camp Nou by 2027 and defending the club against external threats.

item.title: Pesquisa Realtime/Bigdata: Flávio Bolsonaro tem 38% e Lula tem 33% dos votos no Espírito Santo
item.description: A real-time/big data poll conducted in Espírito Santo shows Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) leading with 38% of voter intentions for the October 2026 presidential election, while President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva (PT) trails at 33%. Bolsonaro’s 5-point advantage suggests a competitive race in the state, potentially signaling a shift in the national electoral landscape.

item.title: Lulistas lamentam derrota de ‘O agente secreto’: “CPI do Oscar já”
item.description: Lulist politicians lamented on social media the defeat of the Brazilian film 'O agente secreto' by Kleber Mendonça Filho at the 2026 Oscars, despite four nominations, including Best Actor for Wagner Moura. President Lula (PT) posted a message expressing pride in the film, even though it won no awards at the Sunday, March 15 ceremony.

item.title: A aposta do Centrão sobre o impacto de Vorcaro no calendário pré-eleitoral do Congresso
item.description: Centrão leaders believe the STF's decision to keep Daniel Vorcaro in prison will further empty Congress's pre-election calendar, fostering widespread distrust and ruling out investigative committees on the Master case, which could disrupt the legislative agenda. They argue that releasing Vorcaro would drag them back into the investigation, while his continued imprisonment increases pressure for a potential plea deal, despite his defense denying such intentions.

item.title: Três bons motivos para Neymar ser convocado – e outros três para ele ficar de fora
item.description: The article debates Neymar's potential call-up for Brazil's friendlies against France and Croatia, listing three arguments in favor (his goal-scoring record, enduring respect, and tactical knowledge) and three against (technical decline since 2023, advanced age with reduced physical commitment, and likely refusal to accept a bench role). It underscores the tension between his glorious past and current inconsistent form, questioning whether he still adds value to the team.

item.title: Boulos é desmentido por checagem de fatos ao vivo
item.description: Minister Guilherme Boulos was fact-checked live during an interview on Natal's 98 FM radio when he compared Lula's and Bolsonaro's reactions to investigations targeting their sons. The live fact-check revealed his statement was inaccurate, raising concerns about the reliability of his political comparisons.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro lidera contra Lula em mais um estado do Sudeste, diz Real Time Big Data
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro leads the presidential race against Lula in Espírito Santo with a narrow margin of 1 to 2 percentage points, according to Real Time Big Data. The senator ranges from 38% to 40% in voting intentions, while Lula stands between 33% and 34%. Lula is the most rejected candidate in the state (54%), followed by Bolsonaro (45%).

item.title: A briga entre Ferraço e Pazolini pelo governo do Espírito Santo, segundo o Real Time Big Data
item.description: A Real Time Big Data poll shows a technical tie between Vice Governor Ricardo Ferraço (MDB) and Vitória Mayor Lorenzo Pazolini (Republicanos) in the Espírito Santo gubernatorial race, with both at 35% in one scenario. Ferraço leads in other scenarios, while Senator Magno Malta (PL) is the most rejected candidate. Governor Renato Casagrande (PSB) maintains high approval (79%) and leads the Senate race.

item.title: O último ato de Haddad como ministro antes de embarcar em candidatura ao governo paulista
item.description: Fernando Haddad, the Finance Minister, will step down from his position to run for governor of São Paulo in 2026, at the request of President Lula, with a farewell event scheduled in the state capital. He will serve as a key campaign surrogate for Lula in the state, facing the frontrunner in polls, Tarcísio de Freitas. The ticket remains undefined, but Simone Tebet has been confirmed as his running mate, while Márcio França and Marina Silva are being considered for vice-governor and Senate, respectively.

item.title: Caiu! Tite é demitido e não é mais técnico do Cruzeiro
item.description: Coach Tite was dismissed by Cruzeiro following a draw with Vasco in the 2026 Brazilian Championship, despite the team's recent Minas Gerais state title. The decision by the board, led by Pedro Lourenço, marks a breaking point in the club's season.

item.title: Maurício, do Palmeiras, é convocado para seleção paraguaia
item.description: Palmeiras midfielder Maurício has been called up to the Paraguay national team and will play in friendlies against Greece and Morocco in March. The player, of Paraguayan descent, naturalized in February 2026 and expressed pride in representing La Albirroja.

item.title: Valdemar revela quem Tarcísio quer de vice em SP
item.description: The national president of the PL, Valdemar Costa Neto, denied having asked Eduardo Bolsonaro for help in selecting Tarcísio de Freitas’ running mate for the 2026 elections. Valdemar stated that Tarcísio intends to keep his current vice-governor, Felício Ramuth (PSD), despite PL’s efforts to promote André do Prado, president of the São Paulo Legislative Assembly, as the vice-presidential candidate. He confirmed André accompanied him to the U.S. meeting with Eduardo Bolsonaro but said the vice topic was not discussed.

item.title: França: eleições têm recorde de abstenção e avanço de partidos extremistas
item.description: France's municipal elections recorded the lowest turnout since 1958, with abstention exceeding 40%, signaling growing political polarization. Far-right (National Rally) and far-left (France Unbowed) parties made significant gains, while traditional governing parties were marginalized. The low voter turnout is seen as a warning for the upcoming runoff and the 2025 presidential election.

item.title: Três Graças: confira resumo da semana da novela das nove
item.description: In episodes 127 to 130 of the novel 'Três Graças', the conflict over the valuable sculpture intensifies with revelations of theft, betrayals, and threats of violence. Arminda and Ferente plot to kill Paulinho and Gerluce, while Kasper surprises everyone by revealing the sculpture at his exhibition, exposing Bagdá and Manoel's involvement. Tensions rise with accusations, arrests, and romantic conflicts, especially between Paulinho and Gerluce, whose secret about the sculpture threatens their relationship.

item.title: O timing do governo para começar a destravar a sabatina de Messias
item.description: The Lula government plans to move forward with the confirmation hearing of Jorge Messias, its nominee to the STF, only after the party window ends in April, due to resistance in the Senate. Despite the delay, Lula’s allies state the goal is to finalize the nomination before the electoral period, hoping for a meeting between Lula and Senate President Davi Alcolumbre to break the deadlock. Messias has already met with around 70 senators to gather support.

item.title: Como manutenção de Vorcaro na prisão pode impactar o ritmo dos trabalhos dos Poderes
item.description: The Second Turma of the STF’s decision to maintain Daniel Vorcaro’s imprisonment is expected to affect the pace of work in the Executive and especially the Legislative branches, as deputies return for their sole in-person week amid party window negotiations. The ruling has sparked anxiety in Congress, where lawmakers fear Vorcaro may opt for a plea deal that could expose explosive political ties, prompting cautious behavior. Meanwhile, the Federal Police continue investigations, with eight of Vorcaro’s phones still unexamined, and the STF trial continues until Friday, awaiting the vote of Justice Gilmar Mendes.

item.title: Deputada próxima de Haddad vai liderar oposição a Tarcísio na Alesp
item.description: State deputy Beth Sahão (PT), close to Minister Fernando Haddad and his wife Ana Estela, has assumed the leadership of the minority in the São Paulo Legislative Assembly (Alesp) for 2026. She will lead the opposition against Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicans), criticizing what she calls the 'dismantling of essential public policies,' including cuts to science, restructuring of researchers' careers, and rising gender-based violence. Sahão emphasizes that 2026 will be a crucial year for defending democracy and the rights of vulnerable groups.

item.title: Eleições de 2026: leia as datas e regras do calendário eleitoral
item.description: The 2026 general elections will take place on October 4, with a potential runoff on October 25, under rules published by the TSE on March 4. The electoral calendar sets key deadlines, including voter registration by May 6 and the official start of campaigning on August 16. The TSE presidency will be held by Nunes Marques following Carmen Lucía’s term ending August 25, 2026, with the court composed of STF and STJ justices and lawyers appointed by Lula and the OAB.

item.title: Com base de Lula em guerra no MA, PT debate palanque com prefeito 'isentão'
item.description: The Workers' Party (PT) in Maranhão has begun negotiations for its gubernatorial candidacy with São Luís Mayor Eduardo Braide (PSD), who does not identify as either left or right. The move follows the impossibility of reaching consensus between two Lula allies who despise each other in the state. Braide, who leads in opinion polls, often says he is 'moving forward,' signaling an independent stance.

item.title: Impostos, apoio do pai e Tarcísio fora explicam empate de Flávio com Lula
item.description: The tie between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro in second-round scenarios is explained by early-year tax collection, the formalization of the senator’s candidacy, and Tarcísio de Freitas’ withdrawal from the presidential race, according to experts.

item.title: Brigadeiro revela o fato mais grave do golpe de Bolsonaro, condena Lula e defende reforma na Defesa
item.description: Lieutenant Brigadier Carlos de Almeida Baptista Júnior, former FAB commander, states that responsibility for Brazil’s defense underfunding lies with both the Lula government and Congress, but stresses that investments alone are insufficient without a unified military command. He condemns the 2022 coup attempt, highlighting the most serious breach as the rupture of unified command by Admiral Almir Garnier, and reveals he was threatened by order of General Walter Braga Netto. Baptista Júnior identifies as a liberal right-wing figure and declares he will vote in 2026 for any candidate except Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro, advocating for a break with populism.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro sofre menos ataques no WhatsApp
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) shows the best sentiment balance among himself, Jair Bolsonaro, and Tarcísio de Freitas in public WhatsApp groups, with 56% positive and 44% negative messages. Jair Bolsonaro has a near-perfect balance (50% positive, 50% negative), while Tarcísio has 40% positive and 60% negative. The analysis suggests that support for Jair Bolsonaro partially transfers to Flávio, but hostility toward the former president does not replicate at the same intensity against the son.

item.title: Flávio Bolsonaro lidera crescimento nas redes, e Lula critica dependência digital
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro is growing faster than Lula on social media since announcing his pre-candidacy for president, reflecting a possible technical tie in polls for a potential runoff. Lula has criticized digital dependence, revealing he does not own a cellphone and warning of electoral alienation from such detachment. Experts say his resistance to the digital world may weaken voter connection, while Flávio gains online traction.

item.title: Aliados de Flávio da ala ideológica rechaçam liberais para ministro da Economia
item.description: Presidential pre-candidate Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) stated he will soon announce his economy minister if elected. However, allies from his ideological wing are rejecting market-linked names, favoring profiles more aligned with the conservative and nationalist vision of the Bolsonaro government.

item.title: Após Oscar, Lula diz que 'brasileiros estão orgulhosos do nosso cinema'
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva posted on social media that millions of Brazilians are proud of Brazilian artists' participation at the 2026 Oscars, despite no Brazilian nominee winning. He highlighted 'O Agente Secreto', nominated in four categories, and 'Sonhos de Trem', nominated for cinematography, sharing a montage of the former.

item.title: Los presidenciales juegan sus cartas: Valencia va por el centro, los demás guardan posición
item.description: Paloma Valencia, from the uribist camp, chose a bold strategy with her vice-presidential pick, aiming to shake up the electoral landscape in her favor or against. Sergio Fajardo and Iván Cepeda reinforced more orthodox profiles, while Abelardo de la Espriella seeks to expand his base toward the established right. Claudia López, former Bogotá mayor and lagging in polls, also announced her ticket, but without notable impact so far.

item.title: Brasil permanece orgulhoso, afirma Lula após Oscar não premiar 'O Agente Secreto'
item.description: After the Brazilian film 'O Agente Secreto' failed to win any Oscar in 2026, President Lula stated that the five nominations were already a source of national pride, emphasizing the talent of Brazilian cinema and the strength of its culture. He posted an Instagram montage honoring the cast, including Wagner Moura and Adolpho Veloso, and reiterated that Brazil continues to bring its artistic power to the world.

item.title: Lula celebra indicações de atores brasileiros no Oscar mesmo sem prêmios
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva celebrated Brazil's five Oscar 2026 nominations, expressing pride in the country's cinematic presence despite winning no awards. He specifically praised Kleber Mendonça Filho, Wagner Moura, Gabriel Domingues, and Adolpho Veloso for their work on 'O Agente Secreto' and 'Sonhos de Trem', emphasizing the strength of Brazilian culture on the global stage.

item.title: Episódio mais polêmico da Lava Jato, há 10 anos, barrou Lula em ministério e impulsionou impeachment
item.description: The intercepted phone call between Dilma Rousseff and Lula on March 16, 2016, revealed by Sergio Moro, was interpreted as an attempt to shield Lula from Lava Jato investigations by appointing him Chief of Staff to grant him privileged jurisdiction. The episode triggered mass protests, disrupted congressional sessions, and accelerated Dilma’s impeachment process, becoming a pivotal moment in the political crisis that led to her removal.

item.title: Folha e UOL estreiam Frente a Frente, programa de entrevistas sobre as eleições de 2026
item.description: Folha and UOL launch this Monday (16) the program 'Frente a Frente', a weekly interview series focused on the 2026 elections, hosted by Fábio Zanini and Daniela Lima. The show will air every Monday at 7 PM on UOL's YouTube, UOL Channel, and with broadcast on Folha's homepage. Its goal is to deepen the debate on candidacies and key electoral issues.

item.title: Amapá desponta com mulheres na política e acende debate sobre diferença entre estados
item.description: Amapá could become the Brazilian state that proportionally elects the most women in the 2026 elections if it maintains its performance from the last two general elections. This trend sparks a national debate about regional differences in women's political representation and the factors that either support or hinder female participation across different state contexts.

item.title: Ratinho Júnior, Ronaldo Caiado e Eduardo Leite anunciam 1ª medidas caso ocupem a Presidência
item.description: Paraná Governor Ratinho Júnior, Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado, and Rio Grande do Sul Governor Eduardo Leite — pre-candidates for president from the PSD party — announced their initial policy measures should they be elected, during an interview on TV Band’s Canal Livre program. Their proposals emphasized fiscal adjustments, public security, and administrative efficiency, with a focus on reducing public spending and combating corruption.

item.title: Dirceu critica política externa dos EUA em discurso de aniversário
item.description: Former minister José Dirceu criticized U.S. foreign policy, stating that international conflicts and military interventions threaten the sovereignty of developing nations. He advocated for Brazil to pursue autonomy and diplomatic solutions, praising Lula’s international actions and reaffirming his support for his own pre-candidacy as federal deputy for São Paulo.

item.title: Em discurso, Dirceu afirma que soberania do Brasil está ameaçada
item.description: Former Minister José Dirceu warned that Brazil's sovereignty is threatened by a potential return of Bolsonarism, which could lead to privatization of public companies and reversal of social policies. He advocated for Lula's re-election as essential for a social transformation project, including tax reform targeting large fortunes and elites. Dirceu also confirmed his pre-candidacy for federal deputy from São Paulo in 2026, invited by Lula.

item.title: Pré-candidato ao governo de MG pelo partido do MBL se envolve em briga
item.description: Journalist and pre-candidate for governor of Minas Gerais from the Missão party (linked to MBL), Ben Mendes, was involved in a physical altercation with a store manager in Betim, in the Belo Horizonte metropolitan area. The incident occurred after one of the store’s employees failed to retrieve a product, leading to a confrontation between Mendes and the employee.

item.title: ‘Reconhecimento da potência da cultura brasileira’, diz Lula sobre o Oscar
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva emphasized that Brazil's presence at the Oscars symbolizes the recognition of the strength of national culture, praising Brazilian audiovisual for its talent and international relevance. He cited acclaimed works such as 'City of God', 'Central Station', 'Elite Squad', and 'The Salt of the Earth', and noted that Brazil is competing in five categories this year, including 'The Secret Agent' and Adolpho Veloso's cinematography work in 'Sonhos de Trem'.

item.title: Dirceu diz que Flávio representa “aliança com Trump, guerra e submissão”
item.description: Former Minister José Dirceu (PT) stated that Flávio Bolsonaro’s (PL) presidential pre-candidracy would mean an alliance with Donald Trump, war, and Brazil’s submission, threatening national sovereignty. He accused parts of the Brazilian elite of encouraging a return of Bolsonarism and linked it to institutional risks, calling the January 8, 2023 attack a military coup. Dirceu also confirmed his own pre-candidacy for federal deputy from São Paulo in 2026, invited by Lula.

item.title: José Dirceu associa caso Master à família Bolsonaro
item.description: José Dirceu (PT) claimed, without providing evidence, that the Banco Master case is connected to politicians allied with the Bolsonaro family, directly naming the former president and figures such as Deputy Nikolas Ferreira. He criticized Brazil’s financial system for enabling unsustainable credit operations, such as loans at 150% of the CDI, and argued that right-wing politicians have historically aligned with financial interests. The remarks were made during his 80th birthday speech in São Paulo, with a second celebration planned for Brasília on March 18.

item.title: ‘Estamos diante de eleição que tem Banco Master como maior escândalo’, diz Eduardo Leite
item.description: Rio Grande do Sul governor and presidential pre-candidate Eduardo Leite (PSD) stated that the 2026 election will be defined by the Banco Master billion-dollar fraud scandal as the main corruption case. He promised that, if elected, his first action would be to propose a constitutional amendment to end re-election for executive offices, a practice established in 1997. Leite also disagreed with fellow PSD governors Ronaldo Caiado and Ratinho Júnior on lowering the age of criminal responsibility, arguing that Brazil’s prison system is incapable of absorbing new inmates and requires structural reform before any legal change.

item.title: Ben Mendes e gerente de loja se agridem em briga em MG
item.description: Minas Gerais gubernatorial pre-candidate and digital influencer Ben Mendes (Missão) was involved in a physical altercation with a store manager in Betim after being denied merchandise without a purchase slip. The incident, captured on security footage and shared by Mendes on Instagram, involved mutual aggression including slaps, thrown objects, and threats of violence. Both parties were taken to a medical unit and police station by the Military Police, with Mendes stating he will address the case in an upcoming report on Monday (March 16).

item.title: La izquierda resiste en las grandes ciudades de Francia pese al notable avance de la ultraderecha
item.description: The left maintained its strength in France's major cities — Paris, Marseille, and Lyon — in the 2025 municipal elections, despite significant gains by the far-right National Rally (RN) across the country. RN, led by Marine Le Pen, is seeking to expand its local footprint ahead of the 2027 presidential elections, overcoming a historic weakness in urban centers. The runoff will take place on March 22, with final results expected on Monday.

item.title: Governador do MA faz cópia de jingle de Lula para emplacar sobrinho como sucessor
item.description: Maranhão Governor Carlos Brandão adapted the 'olê, olê, olê, olá, Lula, Lula' jingle to promote his nephew Orleans Brandão (MDB) as his successor, angering the local PT. Despite claims of a prior agreement with the federal government to support Felipe Camarão (PT) and step down in April to run for Senate, Brandão denies any such deal and insists he will serve out his term.

item.title: Sindicato dos Delegados cobra reajuste para Polícia Civil de SP
item.description: The Sindpesp (São Paulo Police Delegates Union) requested a salary increase for civil police officers, stating they are among the lowest-paid in the country and that this affects public security quality. The union notes Governor Tarcísio de Freitas must submit a proposal by April 6 due to electoral law banning raises in the 180 days before the October 6 elections. The request was also sent to state deputies seeking legislative support in Alesp.

item.title: Bolsonaro está 'muito inchado' e 'irritado' na UTI, diz Carlos após visita
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro remains in the ICU at DF Star Hospital in Brasília with worsening kidney function and elevated inflammatory markers, according to a medical bulletin. His son Carlos Bolsonaro reported that the former president is 'very swollen' and 'irritated' due to antibiotics, with limited conversation during the visit. There is no forecast for discharge, and the family is seeking to expedite legal actions to preserve his life, despite obstacles preventing lawyers from visiting.

item.title: Laporta, reelegido presidente del Barcelona con un 68,5% de los votos tras un 74,5% de escrutinio
item.description: Joan Laporta was re-elected president of FC Barcelona with 68.5% of the vote, with 74.5% of ballots counted. Turnout was 48,480 members, or 42.34% of the electorate — the lowest relative turnout since 1997 and the lowest absolute turnout since 2015.

item.title: El PP gana las elecciones y contiene a Vox en Castilla y León mientras el PSOE se recupera
item.description: The elections in Castilla y León showed a strengthening of the PP, which improved its results after nearly 40 years in government and contained Vox's growth, which advanced only modestly. The PSOE also halted its decline and achieved a slight improvement, reinforcing bipartisanship in the region. Nevertheless, the right as a whole maintained its historical dominance, surpassing 54% of the vote.

item.title: Lula, Pedro Almodóvar e outros torcem pelo Oscar para 'O Agente Secreto'; veja
item.description: Brazilian artists and public figures, including President Lula, Anna Muylaert, and Taís Araújo, expressed support for the film 'O Agente Secreto' by Kleber Mendonça Filho on social media ahead of the 2026 Oscar ceremony. Lula emphasized the global recognition of Brazilian culture, while Muylaert highlighted the legacy of Pernambuco cinema and the film as a result of perseverance. The movie is nominated in four categories, including Best Picture and Best Actor for Wagner Moura.

item.title: Pré-candidato do MBL ao governo de MG cai na porrada com gerente de loja. Vídeo
item.description: Ben Mendes, a pre-candidate for governor of Minas Gerais from the Missão party (linked to MBL), was involved in a physical fight with a store manager in Betim, Greater Belo Horizonte, over a dispute regarding the pickup of a porcelain tile order. The incident, captured on video and widely shared on social media, involved mutual aggression including slaps, shoves, bites, and thrown objects, leading both parties to be treated at a UPA and taken to the police station. Mendes claims he was attacked and acted only in self-defense, while the manager alleges she was threatened and assaulted by him.

item.title: França: eleições municipais de Paris, Lyon e Marselha serão decididas no 2° turno, apontam estimativas
item.description: The 2026 municipal elections in Paris, Lyon, and Marseille saw significantly low voter turnout, according to early estimates. No candidate secured an absolute majority in any of the three major cities, indicating that runoff elections will be needed to determine the winners. Polling stations closed on Sunday, March 15, with research institutes highlighting a fragmented and polarized electoral landscape.

item.title: Eleitores cansados dos extremos existem; falta quem os lidere para derrotar a polarização
item.description: Voters tired of the polarization between lulism and bolsonarism represent a significant electoral space for moderate candidates, such as PSD governors (Caiado, Ratinho Júnior or Leite), who bet on stability and unity. Despite elections often being defined by emotional identities, international and national examples show that centrist alternatives can win when perceived as competitive and viable. Success depends on political coordination, broad coalition-building, and early signaling of viability to prevent moderate voters from abandoning the option due to perceived risk.
